name: Burj Khalifa
summary: The Burj Khalifa is the world's tallest building at 828 meters, a super-tall skyscraper that dominates the Dubai skyline. Constructed between 2004 and 2010, it was designed by Adrian Smith of Skidmore, Owings & Merrill and features a Y-shaped floor plan inspired by the Hymenocallis desert flower. The tower holds multiple world records including tallest structure, highest occupied floor, and highest outdoor observation deck.

timeline.title: Construction Begins
timeline.description: Construction of the Burj Khalifa, originally known as Burj Dubai, begins on September 21, 2004. The project is developed by Emaar Properties and designed by Adrian Smith of the architectural firm Skidmore, Owings & Merrill, with the structural engineering led by Bill Baker. The design draws inspiration from the Hymenocallis flower, with three wings, or petals, arranged around a central core to maximize structural stability against wind forces.

timeline.title: Surpasses Taipei 101 as World's Tallest Building
timeline.description: The Burj Khalifa surpasses Taipei 101's 509-meter height to become the world's tallest building, reaching 512 meters. Construction continues at a rapid pace, with approximately one new floor being completed every three days at peak productivity. Over 12,000 construction workers are on site daily, and the building's exterior uses approximately 26,000 glass panels cut individually by hand.

timeline.title: Tallest Man-Made Structure in History
timeline.description: The Burj Khalifa surpasses the 646-meter KVLY-TV mast in North Dakota to become the tallest man-made structure of any kind in history. At this point, the building has reached over 660 meters. The construction employs a sophisticated concrete pumping system that sets a world record by pumping concrete to a height of 601 meters. The project's total concrete volume is approximately 330,000 cubic meters.

timeline.title: Grand Opening Ceremony
timeline.description: The Burj Khalifa is officially inaugurated on January 4, 2010, in a lavish ceremony featuring fireworks, light shows, and a dramatic water fountain display. The building's final height is revealed to be 828 meters with 163 floors, far exceeding all expectations. The tower is renamed Burj Khalifa in honor of Sheikh Khalifa bin Zayed Al Nahyan, President of the UAE, who provided financial support during the 2008 global financial crisis.

timeline.title: Observation Deck Attracts Millions
timeline.description: The Burj Khalifa's observation deck 'At The Top' on the 124th floor becomes one of the world's most popular tourist attractions, welcoming millions of visitors annually. A second observation deck 'At The Top SKY' is opened on the 148th floor at 555 meters, setting the record for the world's highest observation deck. The building also houses the Armani Hotel Dubai, the first Armani hotel in the world, occupying floors 1 through 8 and 38 through 39.

timeline.title: Record-Breaking LED Display
timeline.description: The Burj Khalifa sets a Guinness World Record for the largest LED-illuminated facade, with over 1.1 million LED pixels covering the tower's exterior surface. The facade is used for spectacular light shows during New Year's Eve celebrations and national holidays, becoming an iconic symbol of Dubai's ambition and innovation. The building has become the centerpiece of the Downtown Dubai development, which includes the Dubai Mall and Dubai Fountain.

timeline.title: Continues to Define Dubai's Skyline
timeline.description: Over 14 years after its opening, the Burj Khalifa remains the undisputed tallest building in the world and the defining symbol of Dubai's transformation from a desert trading port to a global metropolis. The building's success has inspired a wave of supertall skyscraper projects worldwide and cemented Dubai's reputation as a city of architectural ambition. Annual visitor numbers to the observation decks exceed 3 million people.

images.caption: The Burj Khalifa rising above the Dubai skyline at sunset, dominating the Downtown Dubai district
The Burj Khalifa under construction, reaching over 600 meters as it surpasses all existing records
The spectacular fireworks display during the Burj Khalifa's grand opening ceremony on January 4, 2010
The Dubai Fountain performing at the base of the Burj Khalifa, with the tower illuminated against the night sky
